ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for cevapcici; 'sausage food product' (FOODON_03302051) is the closest defensible parent class for a skinless ground-meat sausage.
ontology.foodex2_description: Meat products — sausages and similar products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ covers sausages and similar meat products; cevapcici is a skinless grilled sausage and fits within this category as the closest defensible code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for cevapcici or Balkan skinless sausage as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of cevapcici consists of approximately 5 finger-shaped sausages weighing around 150g, served with ajvar relish, chopped raw onions, and flatbread or lepinja.

nutrient_source.title: Cevapcici (Beef) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Cevapcici (Beef), cr_id G00010155, with confidence 0.95. All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for beef sausage (NDB 07064) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented as an adapted field.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on beef sausage / high-protein low-carbohydrate meat product
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cevapcici in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 28 is estimated by analogy with grilled beef sausage and high-protein low-carbohydrate meat products reported in Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Carbohydrate content is 4.19g/100g; GL per 150g serving = (28 × 4.19 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.76, rounded to 1.7. This is a low-GI, low-GL food.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 369mg/100g in a seasoned processed beef product
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit cevapcici to small portions (≤100g) and avoid adding salt or high-sodium condiments, as the sodium content of 369mg/100g can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium limits of 2000mg; potassium at 368mg/100g provides partial cardiovascular offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (4.19g/100g) and high protein (18.48g/100g) supporting glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cevapcici is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions due to its low carbohydrate content (4.19g/100g) and high protein (18.48g/100g), which slows gastric emptying and blunts postprandial glucose response; avoid pairing with high-GI accompaniments such as white bread or sugary sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Red processed meat with saturated fat (~2.4g/100g) and sodium 369mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume cevapcici infrequently and in small portions, as it is a processed red meat product containing saturated fat (~2.4g/100g) and elevated sodium (369mg/100g), both of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk when consumed in excess.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat arachidonic acid content amplifying pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat consumption including cevapcici to no more than once per week, as arachidonic acid from beef promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways that can worsen joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cevapcici (Beef) is a traditional Balkan grilled skinless sausage made from seasoned ground beef, originating from the Western Balkans region encompassing Bosnia, Serbia, and Croatia. The sausages are formed into small finger-shaped cylinders approximately 5–8cm in length, seasoned with garlic, salt, black pepper, and baking soda, then grilled over charcoal or an open flame until lightly charred on the outside and juicy within. The texture is firm yet tender, with a savoury, smoky, and mildly spiced flavour profile. In Singapore, cevapcici is typically served as a restaurant or café dish accompanied by ajvar (a roasted red pepper and eggplant relish), finely chopped raw onions, and homemade flatbread or lepinja. Nutritionally, cevapcici is a high-protein (18.48g/100g), moderate-fat (6.0g/100g), low-carbohydrate (4.19g/100g) food with an energy density of 148 kcal/100g. Its low carbohydrate and high protein content make it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions. The sodium content of 369mg/100g warrants caution for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. As a red processed meat, it is not recommended for frequent consumption by individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or cardiovascular conditions. The glycemic index is estimated at 28 (low), making it a low-glycemic-load food.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010155, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central beef sausage reference. GI estimated by analogy from University of Sydney GI Database approved source. All mandatory fields are complete.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for cevapcici (beef) are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010155) or USDA FoodData Central for this specific Balkan preparation as of 2026. Saturated_fat_g of 2.4g/100g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central beef sausage (NDB 07064) as the closest approved-source equivalent; this is an adapted field. Cultural classification corrected from 'Middle Eastern' (input error) to 'Balkan' based on established culinary origin of cevapcici. GI of 28 is an analogous estimate based on grilled beef sausage and high-protein low-carbohydrate meat products from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables.
